title: Phase 11 — Angular Components, Directives, Forms & Services

questions.question: Which decorator marks a class as an Angular component?
questions.explanation: @Component defines selector, template, and styles.

questions.question: What is the purpose of `selector` in @Component?
options: Element/tag or attribute used to instantiate the component
questions.explanation: selector determines how the component is placed in templates.

questions.question: Which lifecycle hook runs after every change detection when @Input values change?
questions.explanation: ngOnChanges receives SimpleChanges when inputs update.

questions.question: When is `ngOnInit` called relative to the first `ngOnChanges`?
options: After the first ngOnChanges (if inputs exist)
questions.explanation: ngOnInit runs once after the first ngOnChanges.

questions.question: Which hook is best for cleaning up subscriptions?
questions.explanation: ngOnDestroy is the teardown hook.

questions.question: What does `ngAfterViewInit` indicate?
options: Child views and view queries initialized
questions.explanation: View children are available after this hook.

questions.question: What is the difference between a component and a directive?
options: Component has a template; directive augments an existing element
questions.explanation: Components are directives with their own view.

questions.question: What renders with `*ngIf="false"`?
questions.explanation: Structural *ngIf removes the template from DOM when false.

questions.question: What does `*ngFor="let item of items; trackBy: trackId"` optimize?
options: DOM reuse by stable identity when list changes
questions.explanation: trackBy helps Angular identify which items moved/changed.

questions.question: Which is structural directive syntax?
questions.explanation: Asterisk prefix denotes structural directives.

questions.question: How does a parent pass data to a child?
questions.explanation: [prop]="value" binds to child @Input.

questions.question: How does a child notify the parent of an event?
questions.explanation: Child emits via EventEmitter; parent listens with (output)="handler()".

questions.question: Parent template: `<child [count]="n" (changed)="onChange($event)">`. Child has @Output() changed. What is $event?
questions.explanation: $event in the parent is the value emitted by the child.

questions.question: What does `@Input() required: boolean` with `required: true` in Angular 16+ enforce?
options: Compile-time check that parent must bind the input
questions.explanation: Required inputs are validated at compile time in strict templates.

questions.question: Which module provides ngModel?
questions.explanation: FormsModule exports NgModel for template-driven forms.

questions.question: What does `[(ngModel)]="name"` implement?
options: Banana in a box: property + event two-way binding
questions.explanation: [(ngModel)] combines [ngModel] and (ngModelChange).

questions.question: How do you show validation errors for a template-driven control?
questions.explanation: Export ngModel to a template ref to read errors.

questions.question: Which class builds a typed group of controls in code?
questions.explanation: FormBuilder simplifies creating FormGroup hierarchies.

questions.question: Which is valid reactive setup?
questions.explanation: ReactiveFormsModule provides form directives for [formGroup].
questions.code:
this.form = this.fb.group({ email: ['', Validators.required] });
// In component with FormBuilder injected

questions.question: What does `formControlName="email"` require on the ancestor?
questions.explanation: formControlName links to a named control inside parent FormGroup.

questions.question: How do you subscribe to value changes on a FormControl?
questions.explanation: valueChanges is an Observable emitting new values.

questions.question: Which decorator marks a class injectable by Angular DI?
questions.explanation: @Injectable registers the class with the injector.

questions.question: Where should app-wide singleton services be provided in standalone-era best practice?
questions.explanation: providedIn: 'root' registers a tree-shakable singleton.

questions.question: What does `inject(MyService)` do in a functional context (Angular 14+)?
options: Retrieves dependency from current injection context
questions.explanation: inject() resolves tokens during construction/injection context.

questions.question: If ServiceA depends on ServiceB, who creates ServiceB?
options: Angular injector when A is requested
questions.explanation: DI container resolves the dependency graph.

questions.question: What does the `date` pipe do?
options: Formats a date value per locale/format string
questions.explanation: Built-in date pipe formats Date/string/number per pattern.

questions.question: Pure pipes in Angular are called when?
options: When input reference changes or host change detection runs (pure default)
questions.explanation: Pure pipes memoize until input reference changes.

questions.question: How do you declare a custom pipe?
questions.explanation: Custom pipes use @Pipe and implement transform().

questions.question: Template: `{{ price | currency:'USD' }}`. What appears (en-US)?
questions.explanation: currency pipe formats with symbol and decimals per locale.

questions.question: What is `encapsulation: ViewEncapsulation.Emulated` default behavior?
options: Adds unique attributes to scope component CSS
questions.explanation: Emulated encapsulation scopes styles via _ngcontent attributes.

questions.question: What does `[ngClass]="{ active: isActive }"` do?
options: Toggles class active based on isActive
questions.explanation: Object syntax applies keys when values are truthy.

questions.question: Attribute directive `[hidden]` differs from `*ngIf` because?
options: hidden toggles display but element stays in DOM
questions.explanation: hidden uses CSS; *ngIf controls template presence.

questions.question: What does `ngForm` represent?
options: Top-level form directive with aggregate state
questions.explanation: NgForm directive tracks controls registered via ngModel.

questions.question: What is `FormArray` used for?
options: Dynamic list of controls (e.g., repeating inputs)
questions.explanation: FormArray holds an ordered list of AbstractControls.

questions.question: Can you rename an @Input for the template API?
questions.explanation: @Input('alias') exposes a different public property name.

questions.question: Which hook runs on every change detection cycle?
questions.explanation: ngDoCheck fires during every CD run; use carefully for performance.

questions.question: What is a common pitfall with services provided in a component?
options: New instance per component instance (not singleton)
questions.explanation: Component providers create a child injector with new service per component.

questions.question: What does `standalone: true` on a component imply?
options: Component can be bootstrapped/imported without declaring in NgModule
questions.explanation: Standalone components declare their own imports.

questions.question: How do you mark all controls touched to show errors?
questions.explanation: markAllAsTouched propagates touched state.

questions.question: Impure pipe `pure: false` is useful when?
options: Transform depends on mutable state not passed as argument
questions.explanation: Impure pipes run every CD cycle—use sparingly.

questions.question: Microsyntax `*ngFor="let x of list"` desugars to?
questions.explanation: Asterisk wraps content in ng-template with directive instance.

questions.question: Difference between `novalidate` on form and Angular validators?
options: novalidate disables native browser UI; Angular validators still run
questions.explanation: novalidate stops browser popup; Angular handles validation separately.
